subject: Happy birthday, Indiana
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| **_Happy Birthday, Indiana_** This week, we celebrated Indiana’s 201st birthday! And, I have to say, 201 years after our Hoosier pioneers first brought Indiana into the Union, we’ve never looked better.

As **Governor Eric Holcomb** said in his inaugural address nearly a year ago, those pioneers weren’t just people who settled in a place. Today we're still pioneering, coming up with "new ideas or better ways of doing things. They’re trailblazers, inventors, innovators, visionaries.”

And as Hoosiers, we’ve built a state that we can take pride in – a state where Republicans leaders are building our communities into top-ranked places to live, work and play. We’re the home of the No. 1 business climate in the Midwest, and have attracted more Hoosier job commitments this year than ever before in our state’s 201-year history. Indiana is the fiscal envy of the nation, with long-term balanced budgets and the lowest personal income tax rate in the Midwest. We’re a global capital for industries like manufacturing and life sciences, and our state’s entrepreneurs are helping turn Indiana into the Midwest’s technology capital!

Indiana is the best state in the nation because of its people – both the pioneers of the past and the trailblazers of the present. That includes the Hoosiers who step up every day across the state to lead our communities – whether that’s in government, business, schools, local groups, church or at home. You’re helping write our state’s story, which is an epic page-turner that future Hoosiers will be reading for the next 201 years.

-Kyle Hupfer

| This was another big week for Indiana, as **Governor Eric Holcomb** is helping lead Indiana to the record books! This includes helping attract a record-breaking number of new job commitments in a single year -- with companies committing to create 30,158 new Hoosier jobs!

Other big news from Governor Holcomb this week includes:
* Homegrown Indiana tech firm Lessonly [announcing plans](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2001e63e1d&e=1acd9bb3d6) to more than double the size of its workforce, creating 102 new, high-wage Hoosier jobs!
* State employees will soon have [access to paid parental leave](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7e91c757ad&e=1acd9bb3d6), supporting families and healthy kids by ensuring parents -- both women and men -- get the time they need to bond and adjust to a new baby or adopted child.
* The [2021 NBA All Star Game](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6c3dde6110&e=1acd9bb3d6) will be played in Indiana -- a state that knows a lot about basketball and major sporting events.

And, to get you in the holiday spirit, make sure to check out this [viral video of Governor Holcomb](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e2b1411f88&e=1acd9bb3d6) singing a few Christmas tunes in this latest edition of Carpool Karaoke!

| **TAX REFORM UPDATE:
Congress to Vote Next Week**
Congress is scheduled to vote next week on final passage of tax reform -- putting us closer to enacting a simple, competitive and fair tax system for all Americans.

You won't be hearing this from Democrat Senator Joe Donnelly, who voted against tax reform earlier this month, but tax reform is projected to _create 19,721 new jobs_ in Indiana -- and will provide a _meaningful tax cut for middle-class Hoosiers_.

[SIGN](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0bf7365820&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ this petition](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ef47fef417&e=1acd9bb3d6) to show your support for federal tax reform, and visit [PaycheckPresident.com](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bee9433d54&e=1acd9bb3d6) to learn more about this once-in-a-lifetime reform!

subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7a8e897bcd4310a2676ef2465481b7536ef138eb9ff6bd880966c4d12f55fa7d2b976537dffcdcce0d67bb8adba7c532d8
President Trump cuts red tape: On deregulation, winning 22-1

- RealClearPolitics

On Thursday, President Donald J. Trump gave Americans an update on his deregulation agenda in the Roosevelt Room of the White House. The President explained that in his first 11 months in office, his Administration has “canceled or delayed over 1,500 planned regulatory actions, more than any previous president, by far.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7aba00ed52e4f4128cfdd6ab91950297504271174e1b6edc90ed3307ca32629f61374ad90c04853fd33e0cf2c0dcaddc34
A new push against human trafficking on flights

- The Wall Street Journal

The Department of Homeland Security is helping U.S. airports and airlines educate their staffs about how to identify victims of human trafficking—and how to alert authorities without tipping off traffickers. The Wall Street Journal’s extensive report shows that this push is part of a growing effort to thwart human trafficking crimes in the United States.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7a7cfd278bc48c0d211f0814a21c2cb2bf751b3c88063cc7d068aba50cb79200d713d83076b5ba86e3a9c37e8749f5e70d
First lady hopes giving spirit continues through holidays

- The Associated Press

This week, First Lady Melania Trump visited a holiday toy drive sponsored by the U.S. Marine Corps to speak about the spirit of giving during the holiday season. The First Lady challenged Americans to continue pitching in beyond the Christmas holiday and into the new year, especially on behalf of those families affected by the recent hurricanes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7ac0e4beeaa62867573326f2891b09c1e25671efe95e9f78e2b7766957dec5b99e0a4ce7c9aaae75de845eb1101af40988

Trump signs $700B defense policy bill into law, says military has 'gotta be perfecto'

- Washington Examiner

This week President Trump signed the 2018 National Defense Authorization Act, fulfilling his campaign promise of a “major military buildup.” The President also spoke about his “unwavering commitment to our men and women in uniform – the greatest fighting force in the history of the world.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7a851cc50859abf8b4d8ef46c51190f2763b607c4f59c1ef3bb9b5870f9edf3bfbea9e95bb5263bd2750a846bdd393de00
President Trump outpaces Obama with 19 federal court judges confirmed

- The Washington Times

This week, the Senate confirmed President Trump’s 19th federal judge, further boosting the ranks of conservatives throughout the judiciary. By this point in his term, President Obama had only succeeded in having 14 judges confirmed.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7af4ff33c22dfded966a481f53341d3210ec50b139536402f819a8a4b5794b0e4108c534bcf8783d5930b6f68ba40cff52
Latest ICE operation snaps up 100 criminal aliens In New Jersey

- The Daily Caller

U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement recently completed a five-day operation in New Jersey that resulted in the arrest of more than 100 foreign nationals for immigration violations. “Officials said 88 percent those arrested were convicted criminals and 80 percent of them had prior felony convictions for crimes including sexual assault of a minor,” The Daily Caller writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7a4475ac3a6f09ae3292c33f8bf3ce0a94cb20ffdc867f004b48d7b8282cd569f1e08ba51386be6bd3b48f75e6b199c754
Trump garners award from faith leaders for his Jerusalem decision

- The Washington Times

More than 20 faith leaders came to the White House this week to present President Trump with “an award celebrating his support of religious communities and his historic move to recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=19845d4a6aef3e7aa9353f770084a05180cdb54abe65e20be2b660c7d94b49a2974aee23416a0a8bdc024de66418d2e847e47124c33593de
‘On tax cuts, Trump is the modern JFK’

- CNN

Stephen Moore, a CNN economic analyst, writes about the ways that President Trump “seems to share JFK’s wisdom” when it comes to tax cuts. Moore explains that when tax cuts were enacted following President John F. Kennedy’s death, “America experienced one of the greatest periods of prosperity in our history.”

clean_body: 1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS President Trump cuts red tape: On deregulation, winning 22-1 - RealClearPolitics On Thursday, President Donald J. Trump gave Americans an update on his deregulation agenda in the Roosevelt Room of the White House. The President explained that in his first 11 months in office, his Administration has “canceled or delayed over 1,500 planned regulatory actions, more than any previous president, by far.” A new push against human trafficking on flights - The Wall Street Journal The Department of Homeland Security is helping U.S. airports and airlines educate their staffs about how to identify victims of human trafficking—and how to alert authorities without tipping off traffickers. The Wall Street Journal’s extensive report shows that this push is part of a growing effort to thwart human trafficking crimes in the United States. First lady hopes giving spirit continues through holidays - The Associated Press This week, First Lady Melania Trump visited a holiday toy drive sponsored by the U.S. Marine Corps to speak about the spirit of giving during the holiday season. The First Lady challenged Americans to continue pitching in beyond the Christmas holiday and into the new year, especially on behalf of those families affected by the recent hurricanes. Trump signs $700B defense policy bill into law, says military has 'gotta be perfecto' - Washington Examiner This week President Trump signed the 2018 National Defense Authorization Act, fulfilling his campaign promise of a “major military buildup.” The President also spoke about his “unwavering commitment to our men and women in uniform – the greatest fighting force in the history of the world.” President Trump outpaces Obama with 19 federal court judges confirmed - The Washington Times This week, the Senate confirmed President Trump’s 19th federal judge, further boosting the ranks of conservatives throughout the judiciary. By this point in his term, President Obama had only succeeded in having 14 judges confirmed. Latest ICE operation snaps up 100 criminal aliens In New Jersey - The Daily Caller U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement recently completed a five-day operation in New Jersey that resulted in the arrest of more than 100 foreign nationals for immigration violations. “Officials said 88 percent those arrested were convicted criminals and 80 percent of them had prior felony convictions for crimes including sexual assault of a minor,” The Daily Caller writes. Trump garners award from faith leaders for his Jerusalem decision - The Washington Times More than 20 faith leaders came to the White House this week to present President Trump with “an award celebrating his support of religious communities and his historic move to recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.” ‘On tax cuts, Trump is the modern JFK’ - CNN Stephen Moore, a CNN economic analyst, writes about the ways that President Trump “seems to share JFK’s wisdom” when it comes to tax cuts. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
I voted against Mitch McConnell's tax bill because it failed my test: It won't bring more good-paying jobs to Indiana. |
---

| | Peter,

I voted against Mitch McConnell's tax bill because it failed my test: It won't bring more good-paying jobs to Indiana. **And it certainly won 't put more money in the pockets of the Hoosiers who leave for work in the dark and come home in the dark to provide for their families.**

I'm eager to work with anyone who wants to rewrite the tax code to make it better for hardworking middle-class families. But the bill presented to us did the opposite. It raises taxes on the middle class, explodes the deficit, and strips 13 million Americans of their health care.

Now because of my vote, the same Washington special interests who are spending millions in an effort to pass their tax plan are going to come after me -- and they won't wait until next November. The spending could start TODAY.

I have Indiana's back in the Senate, now I hope you'll stand with me, too. **Can you contribute $5 to keep me in the Senate fighting for Hoosier families?**

| Leah held a Health Committee hearing at the state Capitol on Tuesday, and the committee heard testimony on [her bill that would help lower the cost of prescription drugs](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=3x0nGiMjllzNPUzHL5YMTzP5jzsdy__n31-QOXevhhwHx1FdDSLjT9u6pGvmTYpPyJq9Gp4a2eQhjph7sBOlDk2NhXurVYgfBlvmYIJniNjdZXpV4snUdZmtx8-JaMbMDDKmIxrBjhZ3xlW4puDRvpcp2oa7NS6auUxn28j7I1hRpH1PMBrf7PupEppfOBXl7DU2o2d3OJxxRVZ_tJBusXxzk6tullwWj7PgtIE4Kax9C8rhX7yfSK0ENDgm4Bd2qKkeUsg8CzOlE1n7HXRXf5LikCPX_HF8cWUeIlKdDe-54W4GqH5ggS_7xpX7lrmUMd79cMZyPhFj1sAbX9XkLeOa1LLViW2ul9IAKyNzY1U%3D) for seniors and families.
---
| |
---
Leah joined Wisconsin mayors and legislators at the executive residence on Thursday for a very merry Christmas party!

The tree in this picture honors Wisconsin's gold-star families who have lost soldiers for our country.
---
| This week, Leah also signed the U.S. Term Limits pledge that says she would sponsor and vote for a bill in the U.S. Senate that would limit representatives to three terms and senators to two terms.
---
|
---
| Leah's continuing to travel across the entire state to meet with Wisconsinites. Yesterday she met with voters up in Superior, and this week she had events in Wauwatosa and Oconomowoc to meet with concerned citizens.

As always, keep up with Leah as she runs for Wisconsin's U.S. Senate seat by following her [Facebook](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=83xwH31ZzqFUJgB5GBLL17xcJ_cbp3JYDi2evyllzN9G0d_NDbIkRpKEo4d2qZi5N7b4YYGBoQT0owyq7yJUBpw0j-L1isByz-hqTo2vllP9NSmpeFzDSVw0vsPtltYFMgYXvluTX6-DIQYOIWSZCb68BFrLst5eMi8UCaxH0YtIZoTy9MC88b9ANwiox22bqgo3GPeUH9UE4kW2sVezjAbJxbMzSce-d2FRzdTsVzN47aAsniZRWQ172oW4rfMZOpYNfmIESuA0FytLXRxBeQ%3D%3D), [Twitter](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=pskFfowEjbz2PtaUH6543gdptft3IRYma-1aOgeLIfWhJucqxno9xnZzwGprFTIDQ1Ve2JcrlxrX7q2ma7YIDxxU56yCAJ_GUKYJN0Ys8itkOpuyakR7is75BUdV-9Xg1h7JSGzthHcQn3nEsBi95epO_CK7DKjBKzmEDxwzl0Vjy-u80IEuiJom7iW7bPg6n5I2kLLN6n-yRzxceGX9g0Xw5lPn2sdXtqYxx6Cm7JfQFnirbhxYAuQ17lzTvtMsMViEEvx9ic20chmdm5CMhQ%3D%3D) and [Instagram](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=SbTH9NRNum19hyH_r6jqsGEAUSMP97qhvK4nuAeK4JKEffrIXSaEqTCKDmHHNL9OmDZPgl1sqKzplUn0dqksr4popOMDrQGsrhACG9Z_is2hbZf6ndNIySSadwG91RG01sEDMBM4emSxHrFxFFUXs-eE6LwA2aLZ0oScCmp4T8O0LGymx3qbuU2bUVm1udjP3IpXCQED3LTX_MmeCyRQ2q3CNkI0NrVxzXdKV62RpJBxGuBgcLWQ4odw6ol0T099D1K5Mdkubw5ResMRFKZk_A%3D%3D) pages.

## News that drove the week
---
|
## [An Attack on My Privacy in Wisconsin’s Political War](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=PGX0SAiXYjHWtAYogBOg8bvZh_yNMWTpomUk_-qf8d4m1ELiOn0M6JYIyRyaPSlHMO7elOwiLXDP9uKwYFRUonUCi0nEm8X-4CBCQhmPZSXEIYilguj2Opcbn-fcqzaxm6xCxmmS4FE9pXukdwgq9BT8L1Aq0P2gdsBf0YZW_znt7iKUMj-M0tjDwFgvjUV5e5QMr5hOysgMz2KWxKQw5IJT81wPWEtN6entqbx_cAg0gwEH58z4bxwvAHjhMMflxzRwmVuDNnmspllnGaxw8vj6NJVEPw5hwvpglKO33FQyve_v6GN_YJIizQyYyHfa3MKs4lC5-rpQVyybtz69CZY0FFzAerY8C_XQtOf0mrc%3D)
In addition to being a mom and a nurse, I am a conservative state senator. I fear fewer people like me will seek office after seeing what I’ve gone through. Why would a mom want to speak on her political beliefs if she thought it might make her family the target of a government agency? Why would anyone want to become an elected official if it might mean that private conversations are not protected from unreasonable search and seizure?
---
|
## [An Unapologetic Merry Christmas](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=PGX0SAiXYjHWtAYogBOg8YY6eJZK96vwy_yKYsiB9QsunlS42eUvug0uPJrXfv1APmP7eEvID7F3gUFL6MK5cbdY4MhnW8KSZeh7XFBU5Dt8l1BVLDGSuLnpyGF0DI9yz5Ugs9Cb1eMiipztc8O80SlOM3MRKkYqw9lU_hoVCjJLu_o7TzHe34blwadejDW2bh_CXy4h-F5h0bp0_sTXAnbBQnyJe8lIVXRtXzwyjm1qb48uOc7TDUpF8N6kx98hUf18Umrh5PvI6BCgoMttrbGsQCn9vtBU3WRGXK5ls6Setlh9lZm2SuNZNaw9_j5h)
I sincerely doubt those celebrating Hanukkah would take offense to being wished Merry Christmas. In a similar fashion, I would certainly not be offended if wished a Happy Hanukkah. Celebrating and welcoming the traditions of others is part of what makes our country great.

| [ ](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=PGX0SAiXYjHWtAYogBOg8bigjX7hJD15psqwGGMNPp99_asp6ys8zOUPWuHMg8MyBKU_LadfnaxBB8k2pn0FuHQdBGDEFcFP0J9X1qOq5D8jfeWNTIr5njBOLWTyRnMesdGIqhIK_3rehAezmFZ--ca2iCcQaL5RrH9EnRLReWuZjHxU4cYL6lQqJQKiaTToGLB4v28ZWIQubAHQdHXW1KWi93OKK25H_AAgXsRfgnmuo7fyT3bdHG082ozREdJJb-Zu5ClX58RKbVlK1lrSQdXd-tSRfM1V01JPMRVrdjY%3D)

| |

## [H](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=PGX0SAiXYjHWtAYogBOg8YY6eJZK96vwy_yKYsiB9QsunlS42eUvug0uPJrXfv1APmP7eEvID7F3gUFL6MK5cbdY4MhnW8KSZeh7XFBU5DvsKZjKvSAuZBz9a7GaYGteSoONJVFhtyabxV_jckHzKkaXkf80lVp5Q2jEYyWXKcWLIc3tcoKBhh1CdachnH2HuDBa5y2W1Sn5QbOExEyCgs-2PobcHbNujF-YUXNWIc1qPK6QCaMQcGIcv5i08t1ygbWLhY9xFKRHUKdlmjp7JFcmwhZhHAlJ5QOOlfSaYBv4hoaRNiVMSpF7q5pYf5DL)[oward Marklein: I Choose Leah](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=3620891f12144c9d847358fde7ac6e8f&_e=1fz9kzDy9KMtB1YAJsiLLYm4DrxYTwdZOAD8SwGZD06nHcAlaaKFVqVSSWHfJgTTs6tSCINiGpLvc27-Eh3gXOILxhWnugoc6vs3boPFliiWGpXz7td8-aZ5CXegIhETviXdYU9iWZYq4ExzFUkzIg4t1IjlvmWvCLe70vvXrow70xDg-5WYkwhYoTbcY2Q3QyYKZnOaSOKR3CsSrktJ5f64jt9wLHc2EzdYM9MqB-GLjnbBAr63VaeMc2oofjG0zCbNe-SFLA69YerxM7FRl4hg6-f7rLwwLJoNs6pObaOP003iVrOZqluGmyS4XYas_HLI_xqSEuCt2Wkb5I43JBUi8TvxGwNiCoRBPE-NwPWMk2BDExjiQdJpozGB-Q81)

Read her op-ed . | | Momentum for the campaign continued to build this week. Leah received two incredible endorsements from groups dedicated to electing Republican women to Congress. | | Leah held a Health Committee hearing at the state Capitol on Tuesday, and the committee heard testimony on for seniors and families. | | Leah joined Wisconsin mayors and legislators at the executive residence on Thursday for a very merry Christmas party! The tree in this picture honors Wisconsin's gold-star families who have lost soldiers for our country. | This week, Leah also signed the U.S. Term Limits pledge that says she would sponsor and vote for a bill in the U.S. Senate that would limit representatives to three terms and senators to two terms. | | Leah's continuing to travel across the entire state to meet with Wisconsinites. Yesterday she met with voters up in Superior, and this week she had events in Wauwatosa and Oconomowoc to meet with concerned citizens. As always, keep up with Leah as she runs for Wisconsin's U.S. Senate seat by following her , and pages. Have a great weekend! Team Leah | News that drove the week | In addition to being a mom and a nurse, I am a conservative state senator. I fear fewer people like me will seek office after seeing what I’ve gone through. Why would a mom want to speak on her political beliefs if she thought it might make her family the target of a government agency? Why would anyone want to become an elected official if it might mean that private conversations are not protected from unreasonable search and seizure? | I sincerely doubt those celebrating Hanukkah would take offense to being wished Merry Christmas. In a similar fashion, I would certainly not be offended if wished a Happy Hanukkah. Celebrating and welcoming the traditions of others is part of what makes our country great. | Watch Leah on with Mike Gousha's show on Sunday . | | "As a registered nurse, she represents the health care community as well as women throughout the state with vigor. She is an intelligent, savvy, astute leader who will do the same for all of us. She shares our values and we need these values to be represented in the U.S. Senate.
